title: Ev Charger Network Expansion Pace Acceleration
thesis: Rapid week-over-week growth in available public EV chargers signals infrastructure investment confidence and forward demand expectations for electric vehicles. EV charger buildout is industrial capex spending and signals consumer EV adoption momentum, benefiting industrials and energy transition plays.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API weekly charger count by state/region through openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: If weekly new charger count exceeds 200-unit increase over prior week average, triggering bullish infrastructure signal
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly new charger count exceeds 200-unit increase over prior week average, triggering bullish infrastructure signal Exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly charger additions fall back below 100-unit threshold
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API weekly charger count by state/region through openchargemap adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger networks expand in spurts tied to state/federal incentive cycles; growth accelerations occur multiple times per quarter.

schema.idea: EV Charger Network Expansion Pace Acceleration

data.source: OpenChargeMap API weekly charger count by state/region through openchargemap adapter

schema.behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in available public EV chargers signals infrastructure investment confidence and forward demand expectations for electric vehicles.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger buildout is industrial capex spending and signals consumer EV adoption momentum, benefiting industrials and energy transition plays.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly new charger count exceeds 200-unit increase over prior week average, triggering bullish infrastructure signal
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly charger additions fall back below 100-unit threshold

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger networks expand in spurts tied to state/federal incentive cycles; growth accelerations occur multiple times per quarter.

title: Google Trends Spike In Appliance Shortage Searches
thesis: Sharp spikes in appliance shortage and delivery delay searches indicate supply chain friction and consumer frustration, signaling imminent price pressures and margin compression. Appliance shortage spikes foreshadow consumer discretionary spending pullback and retail margin pressure.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' + 'appliance delivery delay' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly trend volume exceeds 2-week rolling average by 40% or more
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly trend volume exceeds 2-week rolling average by 40% or more Exit: After 7 trading days or when trend volume falls below 20% above rolling average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' + 'appliance delivery delay' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain volatility creates recurring appliance shortage search spikes; typical fire rate 2-4 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Google Trends Spike in Appliance Shortage Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' + 'appliance delivery delay' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Sharp spikes in appliance shortage and delivery delay searches indicate supply chain friction and consumer frustration, signaling imminent price pressures and margin compression.

market_impact.rationale: Appliance shortage spikes foreshadow consumer discretionary spending pullback and retail margin pressure.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly trend volume exceeds 2-week rolling average by 40% or more
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or when trend volume falls below 20% above rolling average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain volatility creates recurring appliance shortage search spikes; typical fire rate 2-4 times per quarter.

title: Freight Density Index Via Google Trends Logistics Job Searches
thesis: Elevated logistics job search volume reflects tight freight capacity and rising demand for shipping services, signaling inflationary pressure on transport costs. Rising logistics job search volume correlates with freight rate spikes and industrial transport demand strength.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'truck driver job hiring' + 'logistics coordinator job' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly trend volume for logistics job searches exceeds 3-week average by 35%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly trend volume for logistics job searches exceeds 3-week average by 35% Exit: After 10 trading days or if trend falls below rolling average by 10%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'truck driver job hiring' + 'logistics coordinator job' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal and cyclical freight demand creates recurring spikes in job search volume; fires 2-3 times per month on average.

trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if trend falls below rolling average by 10%

title: Healthcare Cost Shock Via Google Trends Medical Bill Searches
thesis: Spikes in medical bill and payment plan searches indicate consumer healthcare cost shock, signaling financial distress in lower-income cohorts. Healthcare cost shock reduces discretionary spending capacity and pressures consumer staples and retail margins.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill payment plan' + 'hospital bill negotiation' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly trend volume exceeds 4-week rolling average by 50%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly trend volume exceeds 4-week rolling average by 50% Exit: After 8 trading days or when trend normalizes below 25% above rolling average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill payment plan' + 'hospital bill negotiation' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Medical billing cycles and insurance deductible resets create recurring spikes; typical frequency 3-4 times per quarter.

title: Rental Market Heat Via Google Trends Apartment Search Surge
thesis: Spikes in apartment rental and rent price searches indicate housing market tightness and inflation in residential costs, signaling wealth squeeze. Rising rental cost pressure reduces discretionary income and consumer spending capacity.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'apartment rentals near me' + 'rent prices' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly trend volume for apartment searches exceeds 3-week average by 45%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly trend volume for apartment searches exceeds 3-week average by 45% Exit: After 12 trading days or if trend falls back to rolling average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'apartment rentals near me' + 'rent prices' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal rental cycles (spring/summer, year-end) create predictable spikes; fires 3-5 times per quarter.

title: Cybersecurity Crisis Sentiment Via News Rss Feed Volume
thesis: Spikes in cybersecurity breach and vulnerability news article counts signal heightened security incident activity, triggering defensive rotation and IT spending urgency. Cybersecurity crises drive emergency IT and security software budget allocation, benefiting tech security vendors.
data_sources: RSS feed article count weekly from major tech/security news sources (Ars Technica, SecurityWeek, Dark Reading) filtered for 'zero-day' + 'vulnerability' + 'breach' keywords through rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If weekly article count from security RSS feeds exceeds 2-week average by 60%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly article count from security RSS feeds exceeds 2-week average by 60% Exit: After 7 trading days or if article count drops below 30% above average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed article count weekly from major tech/security news sources (Ars Technica, SecurityWeek, Dark Reading) filtered for 'zero-day' + 'vulnerability' + 'breach' keywords through rss_count adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly cybersecurity incidents and vulnerability disclosures occur constantly; fire rate typically 2-3 times per week.

market_impact.rationale: Cybersecurity crises drive emergency IT and security software budget allocation, benefiting tech security vendors.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly article count from security RSS feeds exceeds 2-week average by 60%
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if article count drops below 30% above average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly cybersecurity incidents and vulnerability disclosures occur constantly; fire rate typically 2-3 times per week.

title: Airline Load Factor Drop Signal Via Bts Proxy Data
thesis: Declining airline load factors indicate weakening travel demand and reduced pricing power, signaling consumer discretionary spending pullback. Load factor declines correlate with consumer leisure travel pullback and discretionary income squeeze.
data_sources: BTS monthly airline load factor (Revenue Passenger-Miles / Available Seat-Miles) through bts_airline_load_factor adapter
signal_logic: If 4-week rolling load factor drops 2% or more below prior 12-week average
entry_exit: Entry: If 4-week rolling load factor drops 2% or more below prior 12-week average Exit: After 12 trading days or when load factor stabilizes above prior average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS monthly airline load factor (Revenue Passenger-Miles / Available Seat-Miles) through bts_airline_load_factor adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel demand swings create regular load factor oscillations; typical fire rate 2-3 times per quarter.

data.source: BTS monthly airline load factor (Revenue Passenger-Miles / Available Seat-Miles) through bts_airline_load_factor adapter

schema.behavior: Declining airline load factors indicate weakening travel demand and reduced pricing power, signaling consumer discretionary spending pullback.

market_impact.rationale: Load factor declines correlate with consumer leisure travel pullback and discretionary income squeeze.

trade_logic.entry: If 4-week rolling load factor drops 2% or more below prior 12-week average
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or when load factor stabilizes above prior average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel demand swings create regular load factor oscillations; typical fire rate 2-3 times per quarter.

title: Commodity Input Pressure Via Fred Ppi Spike
thesis: Weekly spikes in crude materials PPI indicate upstream cost inflation, signaling margin pressure on industrials and energy-intensive sectors. Rising raw material costs compress manufacturing margins and signal inflationary headwinds.
data_sources: FRED series: Producer Price Index for Crude Materials (PPICRM) weekly through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If weekly PPI reading exceeds 4-week rolling average by 1.5% or more
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly PPI reading exceeds 4-week rolling average by 1.5% or more Exit: After 10 trading days or when PPI normalizes within 0.5% of rolling average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series: Producer Price Index for Crude Materials (PPICRM) weekly through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity prices fluctuate weekly; PPI spikes occur 2-4 times per month during volatile periods.

schema.idea: Commodity Input Pressure via FRED PPI Spike

data.source: FRED series: Producer Price Index for Crude Materials (PPICRM) weekly through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: Weekly spikes in crude materials PPI indicate upstream cost inflation, signaling margin pressure on industrials and energy-intensive sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Rising raw material costs compress manufacturing margins and signal inflationary headwinds.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly PPI reading exceeds 4-week rolling average by 1.5% or more
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or when PPI normalizes within 0.5% of rolling average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity prices fluctuate weekly; PPI spikes occur 2-4 times per month during volatile periods.

title: Earthquake Insurance Demand Surge Via Google Trends
thesis: Spikes in earthquake insurance searches following seismic activity indicate regional economic shock and risk re-evaluation, signaling local property market repricing. Earthquake insurance demand spikes benefit insurance and reinsurance company underwriting revenue and premiums.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'earthquake insurance' + 'home insurance after earthquake' through google_trends adapter, cross-referenced with USGS earthquake activity feed through earthquake_activity adapter
signal_logic: If USGS detects 4.0+ magnitude earthquake in populated region AND Google Trends volume for earthquake insurance exceeds 3-week average by 70% within 2 days
entry_exit: Entry: If USGS detects 4.0+ magnitude earthquake in populated region AND Google Trends volume for earthquake insurance exceeds 3-week average by 70% within 2 days Exit: After 14 trading days or if search volume normalizes
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'earthquake insurance' + 'home insurance after earthquake' through google_trends adapter, cross-referenced with USGS earthquake activity feed through earthquake_activity adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity in North America and Pacific regions occurs regularly; significant earthquakes trigger insurance search spikes 3-5 times per year, with weekly opportunities.

schema.idea: Earthquake Insurance Demand Surge via Google Trends

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'earthquake insurance' + 'home insurance after earthquake' through google_trends adapter, cross-referenced with USGS earthquake activity feed through earthquake_activity adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in earthquake insurance searches following seismic activity indicate regional economic shock and risk re-evaluation, signaling local property market repricing.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake insurance demand spikes benefit insurance and reinsurance company underwriting revenue and premiums.

trade_logic.entry: If USGS detects 4.0+ magnitude earthquake in populated region AND Google Trends volume for earthquake insurance exceeds 3-week average by 70% within 2 days
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days or if search volume normalizes

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity in North America and Pacific regions occurs regularly; significant earthquakes trigger insurance search spikes 3-5 times per year, with weekly opportunities.
